health_details.gemini_analysis_summary: The battery displays excellent electrochemical stability with a low cycle count and consistent voltage-to-SOC correlation. Thermal management has been optimal, staying well below degradation thresholds, suggesting a highly conservative primary usage profile.

target_system.name: ReVolt R-48 High-Efficiency NMC Storage Unit
target_system.topology_explanation: Based on the telemetry range (371.7V–399V), this is a 96S NMC architecture. To achieve a 48V nominal system for residential use, we are harvesting a 14S segment. 14S provides a 51.8V nominal and 58.8V peak voltage, which perfectly aligns with standard 48V hybrid solar inverters.

module_assessment.reason: SOH is 94.5% with no thermal anomalies; cell deviation is within 15mV across the string.
module_assessment.notes: Excellent health. These cells show no sign of lithium plating or separator fatigue.

required_tools.purpose: Safe removal of busbars and casing bolts.
Verifying step-by-step voltage drops and circuit continuity.
Ensuring gas-tight connections for the new 48V main leads.

required_parts.purpose: Cell balancing, over-voltage/under-voltage protection, and SOC reporting.

upcycle_steps.title: Establish High-Voltage Perimeter
upcycle_steps.instruction: Clear the bench of all conductive materials. Ensure the technician is wearing Class 0 insulated gloves. Verify the Pack Service Plug is removed to break the internal circuit.
upcycle_steps.expected_reading: 0V across main terminals after plug removal
upcycle_steps.safety_warning: The pack is currently at ~399V. This is lethal DC voltage. Do not proceed until gloves are air-tested for leaks.
upcycle_steps.voice_agent_note: Alright, let's start safe. Clear the deck and get those gloves on. Double-check that service plug is pulled before we even think about opening the lid.

upcycle_steps.title: Top Cover Removal
upcycle_steps.instruction: Unscrew the 24 perimeter bolts using a 10mm insulated socket. Lift the cover using the designated hoist points. Do not pry the lid with a screwdriver as it may puncture the pouch cells.
upcycle_steps.safety_warning: Internal busbars are exposed once the lid is removed. Watch your tool clearance.
upcycle_steps.voice_agent_note: Easy does it with the lid. We don't want any metal tools slipping into those modules. Once it's off, just take a second to look for any loose debris.

upcycle_steps.title: Confirm Individual Cell Voltages
upcycle_steps.instruction: Measure from Cell 1 Positive to Cell 1 Negative. Repeat for the first 14 cells in the string. Record each value.
upcycle_steps.safety_warning: Keep multimeter probes steady. Shorting a cell terminal to the chassis will cause an immediate arc flash.
upcycle_steps.voice_agent_note: We're looking for consistency here. If any cell is more than 0.05V off the others, we need to pause and check for a parasitic load.

upcycle_steps.title: Isolate the 14S String
upcycle_steps.instruction: Using the insulated wrench, remove the busbar connecting Cell 14 to Cell 15. This creates the break for our 48V sub-string. Label the new negative terminal as 'System GND'.
upcycle_steps.safety_warning: The remaining 82 cells in the pack are still live. Insulate the exposed terminals of Cell 15 immediately with Kapton tape.
upcycle_steps.voice_agent_note: We're basically performing surgery here. Once you cut that connection between 14 and 15, tape up the 'hot' side immediately. We only care about our first 14 cells now.

upcycle_steps.title: Wire Balance Leads
upcycle_steps.instruction: Connect the BMS balance harness starting from B- (System GND) then B1, B2, through to B14. Follow the color-coded schematic provided by the BMS manufacturer.
upcycle_steps.expected_reading: BMS app should show 14 distinct voltages
upcycle_steps.safety_warning: Incorrect wiring order will destroy the BMS microchip instantly.
upcycle_steps.voice_agent_note: Take your time with the balance wires. Count them out loud: zero, one, two... all the way to fourteen. Check the harness plug with your meter before you actually plug it into the BMS.

upcycle_steps.title: Main Power Leads
upcycle_steps.instruction: Crimp 00 AWG lugs onto the main discharge cables. Connect the BMS 'B-' lead to the pack negative and 'C-' to the system output terminal.
upcycle_steps.safety_warning: Ensure all lugs are torqued to 12Nm. Loose connections cause heat and fires.
upcycle_steps.voice_agent_note: Crimp them tight. A loose lug is a heater, and we're building a battery, not a toaster.

upcycle_steps.title: Final Voltage and Communication Check
upcycle_steps.instruction: Power on the BMS via the physical switch. Open the ReVolt OS diagnostic app on your tablet and pair via Bluetooth. Check for any 'High Voltage' or 'Cell Imbalance' alarms.
upcycle_steps.safety_warning: Do not bypass the BMS if an error code is present.
upcycle_steps.voice_agent_note: Let's see what the brain says. If the app shows green across the board, we're in business. Look at those cell deltas—they should be nice and tight.

pre_upcycle_checklist: Verify Class 0 gloves are within inspection date.
Confirm 48V BMS is programmed for NMC chemistry (4.2V Max, 3.0V Min).
Ensure fire suppression (Class D or specialized EV fire blanket) is on standby.
Validate that the workspace is ESD-safe.

post_upcycle_verification.method: Apply a 50A load using a DC load bank until voltage reaches 44.8V (3.2V/cell).
post_upcycle_verification.fail_action: Check for individual cell sag; replace specific weak cells if necessary.

post_upcycle_verification.method: Run at 100A discharge for 30 minutes while monitoring with FLIR thermal camera.
post_upcycle_verification.fail_action: Re-torque busbars and check crimp quality.

upcycle_blueprint.gemini_engineering_notes: The high SOH (94.5%) and low cycle count indicate this pack was likely a warranty pull for a non-battery issue or a low-mileage salvage. Utilizing a 14S configuration maximizes the usable SOC window for residential solar while staying within the safety limits of standard 48V inverter hardware. The 96S native architecture allows for exactly six 48V modules to be harvested from a single donor pack if desired.

listing.title: Grade A EV Battery Module | 94.5% SOH | 312 Cycles | Low Thermal Stress
listing.description: Premium second-life battery module certified for industrial storage. Features minimal cyclic wear and zero high-current stress events, making it ideal for high-reliability solar backup applications.

safety_workflow.target_config: 14S1P ReVolt R-48 High-Efficiency NMC Storage Unit
